summary: Marketing surfaces for LLM-powered products and autonomous agent platforms. Audience is mixed — technical buyers and curious operators — looking for evidence that the agent works on a representative task.

patterns.name: Embedded agent demo
patterns.summary: Hero anchored by a chat- or transcript-shaped demo panel. The demo shows a representative input on top and an agent response below, scoped to a fixed example.

variants.summary: Static transcript, no animation. Safe default.
Replays the example in 3–5 timed steps with reduced-motion respected.
User can edit the prompt and re-run against a sandboxed example.

anti_examples: A generic chat input that does not show output is not this pattern — see hero/single-prompt-input.

patterns.name: Before / after panel
patterns.summary: Two stacked or side-by-side panels showing the same task before and after the agent. Frames the value proposition as an outcome diff, not a feature list.

variants.summary: Two columns of equal width with subtle dividers.
Vertical stack, useful when the 'before' state is text-heavy.

patterns.name: Agent workflow diagram
patterns.summary: A linear or directed-graph diagram representing the agent's steps (plan → act → review). Annotated nodes describe each step with one short caption.

variants.summary: Left-to-right pipeline of 3–5 boxes connected by arrows.
A pipeline that loops back, for self-critique or iterative agents.

anti_examples: Pure illustration without labels is excluded; this pattern requires per-node captions.

patterns.name: Role-based use-case grid
patterns.summary: A grid of 3–6 cards, each titled by a role or function (e.g. 'Sales operations', 'Support'), with a 2–3 line description of how the agent applies.

variants.summary: Cards titled by workflow name (e.g. 'Lead enrichment').

patterns.name: Safety and controls block
patterns.summary: A dedicated section listing controls — guardrails, audit logs, human approval, data isolation. Often presented as 4 small cards with monochrome iconography.

variants.summary: Four icon-led cards in one row.
A checked list with short labels and 1-line descriptions.

patterns.name: Evaluation metric strip
patterns.summary: A row of 3–4 numeric claims tied to evaluation outcomes (accuracy on a named benchmark, escalation rate, average resolution time) with explicit units and a baseline note.

variants.summary: Each metric has a small 'vs baseline X' caption.

anti_examples: Vague 'X% faster' claims without a benchmark are excluded — see anti-clone-policy on hollow metrics.

patterns.name: Dual CTA with trust line
patterns.summary: A conversion band with two CTAs ('Try it' and 'Talk to us') above a one-line trust statement (e.g. 'No credit card. SOC 2 in progress.').

variants.summary: Centered heading with stacked CTAs and the trust line beneath.
Left-aligned with CTAs to the right and trust line under the heading.
